title: VIP vs. Bulk Passenger Ratio
query: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN PassengerType IN ('VIP', 'Tourist') THEN PassengerCount ELSE 0 END) AS vip, SUM(CASE WHEN PassengerType NOT IN ('VIP', 'Tourist') THEN PassengerCount ELSE 0 END) AS bulk FROM MissionAccepted WHERE PassengerMission = 1 AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate

details.text: {vip} VIP/luxury passenger(s) vs. {bulk} bulk/economy passenger(s) transported during the report period. This split shows whether you're running a boutique charter operation or a mass-transit service — the two require different ships, different routes, and a completely different relationship with your clientele.
details.help: PassengerType VIP and Tourist are grouped as premium; others as bulk. vip drives the title as the prestige passenger count.

tagLines: Passenger class distribution analysis compiled. VIP vs. bulk transport ratio noted in the mission record.
Luxury passengers pay more and demand more. Bulk passengers fill the hold and keep quiet. Usually.
There's a version of this career that's all champagne and gowns. Then there's the actual route economics.
